description_long: This object consists of a group of traditional buildings arranged in a rectangular grid, each featuring prominent gabled roofs with upturned eaves, characteristic of East Asian architecture. The roofs are dark gray, likely tiled, and extend outward with a slight curvature, providing a sense of grandeur and symmetry. The walls are primarily white with red wooden framing, forming a grid pattern that is both decorative and structural. The buildings are closely spaced, separated by narrow gaps, and lack visible windows or doors from these angles, emphasizing their uniformity and collective presence. The overall design suggests a historical or cultural site, possibly a temple complex or heritage attraction. The architectural style and layout evoke a sense of historical significance and communal function.
description: A cluster of traditional East Asian-style buildings with dark gabled roofs and red-and-white framed walls, arranged in a symmetrical grid. The design is ornate and evokes a historical or cultural landmark.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical arrangement of the buildings, with prominent gabled roofs and red-and-white facades.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated shape of each building and the repeating pattern of the roof ridges.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the same grid of buildings and visible roof and wall details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the length and uniformity of the buildings' roofs.
